zotero.abstractNote: Ensemble methods are a cornerstone of modern machine learning, yet their robustness properties remain poorly understood. In this work, we propose a general certification theory for ensembles based on the notion of S-Lipschitzness, which allows us to derive certified robustness guarantees for a wide range of ensemble architectures and aggregation functions. Our theory encompasses both randomized and deterministic ensembles, and provides a unified framework for analyzing their robustness. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach by certifying several state-of-the-art ensemble models on standard benchmarks, showing that our bounds are significantly tighter than existing methods.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 40th International Conference on Machine Learning
